summary.intent: Fix code review comments on PR #498 in the moltis repo, specifically around `parse_chat_target` error handling and typing indicator routing in the Telegram crate.
summary.outcome: Fixed `parse_chat_target` to return an error instead of silently converting unparseable thread_id to `None`, committed and pushed the fix to `origin/oasis-cattle`.

repo: Telegram forum-topic session isolation is handled in `crates/telegram/src/topic.rs` via `parse_chat_target`.
PR #498 is on branch `oasis-cattle` in the `moltis-org/moltis` repo.
Greptile is used as an automated code review bot on this repo's PRs.

code.finding: `parse_chat_target` was using `.parse::<i32>().ok()` which silently converts parse errors to `None` — fixed to return a proper `Result` error instead.

workflow: Greptile review comments appear on the PR body, not as inline review threads, so they cannot be resolved via the GitHub API thread-resolution endpoint.
Replying to the Greptile comment was used as a proxy for 'resolving' the conversation since no thread API was available.

friction: Greptile posts findings as PR body comments rather than inline review threads, making programmatic resolution impossible — required a manual reply workaround.
